title: After months of backtesting and refinement, I finally switched my algo to live trading this Monday.
author: Nearby_Option_6300

comments.author: paulf280
comments.body: After all the backtesting and refinement, what were the results? Hope it went well

replies.author: Nearby_Option_6300
replies.body:
Thanks! Honestly the backtesting *was* most of the work — 2+ years of real NIFTY + SENSEX data (not synthetic). It looked promising, but I'll be straight with you: backtests *always* look better than live — it's incredibly easy to overfit — and the data had plenty of red days and losing setups too. It's not some magic all‑green system.

That's exactly why I'm posting the **live** numbers day by day instead of a glossy backtest curve. Week 1 live is up \~57% on the account with 3/3 green days — but it's *4 days*, so I'm reading basically nothing into it yet. The real test is months, and how it behaves in the drawdowns. So far the risk discipline (hard stops, stop‑after‑loss) has held, which matters more to me than the return. I'll keep posting the raw numbers — good *and* bad.

comments.author: paulf280
comments.body: That's brilliant, well fucking done. Great to hear people like yourself making it work, the backtesting and refinement puts years on you(i know only too well) but when it clicks its great to see. Keep posting the numbers, well done my friend 💪

replies.author: Nearby_Option_6300
replies.body: Appreciate that, truly 🙏 You know the grind then — months of backtesting that feel pointless until they suddenly click. Still early for me and expecting red weeks ahead, but this makes it worth it. Thanks friend, good luck with yours 💪
